name: hero-section
role: Top-level <section> wrapper for the unified hero pattern. Carries the universal attributes (data-variant, data-alignment, data-size, data-background) that select variant + alignment + scale + surface. Consolidated from the 5 prior per-variant section primitives (hero-section / hero-animated-section / hero-mesh-section / hero-photo-section / hero-video-section) per Phase 22 Consolidation 3.

variant.note: Selects which conditional slots are activated and which variant-specific attributes apply.

alignment.note: Universal content-alignment attribute. Replaces deprecated data-register (Cons. 3 Q2).

background.note: Surface treatment. Effective for type-only variant primarily; image-led variants (brand-photo, video) use media + overlay instead.

meta.consolidationNote: All 5 prior section primitives collapse to this single hero-section. Variants are selected via data-variant attribute.

name: hero-eyebrow
role: Small uppercase pre-heading above the main headline. Used to categorise ('PRODUCT LAUNCH') or signal context ('NEW IN GALLEY').

tone.note: on-image / on-mesh / on-video adjust contrast for variants where eyebrow sits over media/mesh rather than surface.

ariaPatterns.notHeading:
Eyebrow is NOT a heading element — it's

. The h1 is hero-headline below.

name: hero-headline
role: Display-tier headline. The h1 anchor for marketing pages. Token-sized by data-size on the parent hero-section.

shadow.note: True only for video variant (text-shadow for legibility against motion). Applied via [data-variant=video][data-shadow=true] selector.

meta.h1Discipline: Hero-headline is the page's single h1. Don't add h1 elsewhere on the page.

name: hero-subhead
role: Supporting subtitle paragraph below the headline. 1-2 sentences max.

background-tint.note: Subtle inline backdrop tint used in gradient-mesh dramatic intensity to preserve WCAG AA contrast over bright mesh.

name: hero-actions
role: CTA cluster. **Composes <action-button> from @quoin/pattern-button-system** (Cons. 3 Q5). Hero adds context-scoped backdrop-blur overrides on ghost buttons when sitting over media (image-full-bleed / image-overlay layouts; video variant).

cluster.note: Renamed from prior 'data-register' for clarity (Cons. 3 Q2).

meta.composedAs: <button class='action-button' data-intent='...'> OR <a class='action-button' data-intent='...' href='...'> (polymorphic; the action-button primitive renders as anchor when href provided)
meta.noInlinedCta: The prior 5 hero packs each declared their own .cta CSS class duplicating button-system. Cons. 3 removes that duplication. Hero CSS does not declare .cta; the action-button class lives in button-system. Hero only adds context-scoped overrides for the backdrop-blur ghost variant on media backgrounds.

name: hero-meta
role: Optional trust line below the CTAs. Disclaimers / social-proof / trust-badges.

kind.note: Renamed from prior 'data-register' for clarity (Cons. 3 Q2).

ariaPatterns.trustBadgeAltText: Trust-badge images must carry alt-text naming the certification — not aria-hidden.

name: hero-accent
role: Ambient decorative motion behind the hero content (pulse / drift). Hidden under prefers-reduced-motion.

kind.note: Renamed from prior 'data-register' for clarity (Cons. 3 Q2).

meta.reducedMotion: display: none under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. Not slowed — stopped entirely.

name: hero-background
role: Procedural OKLCH radial-gradient mesh background. CSS Color Module Level 4. @supports falls back to solid var(--accent-recede) on unsupported browsers.

name: hero-media
role: Visual asset slot. <picture> for brand-photo (responsive AVIF/WebP/JPEG srcset). <video autoplay muted loop playsinline> for video.

aspect.note: Used in brand-photo's image-right / image-left layouts.

focal-point.note: Object-position. Used in brand-photo for art direction; video uses center.

meta.altRequirements: Brand-photo's must carry descriptive alt-text. Video's
meta.lcpOptimization: fetchpriority='high' + decoding='async' for the largest contentful paint candidate.

name: hero-overlay
role: Scrim for text-legibility over media. Three styles × two tones × three strengths = 18 overlay variants.

kind.note: Renamed from prior 'data-register' for clarity (Cons. 3 Q2).

meta.localToken: --overlay-strength scoped to the variant via [data-variant=brand-photo] / [data-variant=video] selector.

name: hero-controls
role: WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) mandatory pause control for background-video variant. Renders as <button>. Positioned in one of 4 corners.

meta.wcag: 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide — non-optional for data-variant=video. Auto-playing background video without a visible pause control violates 2.2.2.
meta.stateBinding: Parent hero-section's data-state toggles between 'playing' and 'paused'. The control's aria-label and visible glyph respond to that state. State transition triggered by user click on the control.
